conversations.say: Frankie, I accept your qualified account and your protection offer. Night Desk material passed through a shared drop and multiple contributors worked it; neither fact identifies R.
conversations.offer: I will provide bounded, non-identifying workflow context for confidential legal review.
conversations.request: Proceed with independent legal review for contributor protection and preserve both the desk-receipt account and expense-record contradiction in any public account.

conversations.say: Jules, you accepted the conditions publicly. Night Desk will support your provisional stewardship while those safeguards hold.
conversations.offer: I will fulfill collective support for non-exclusive stewardship.
conversations.request: Keep contributor consent, collective review, and explicit labor credit as continuing conditions—not ceremonial promises.

conversations.say: Kit, I will join the comparison, but only as a bounded witness to collaborative production. Night Desk cannot supply your transition for you.
conversations.offer: I will describe shared-drop workflow and collaborative labor without identifying contributors.
conversations.request: Repeat that this supports a production stage, not a human R, an actual transition, later autonomous control, or Vale's fate.

public_statement: Night Desk material passed through a shared drop, and different people researched, translated, checked, or rewrote work later published as R. Frankie, Jules, and Maren have publicly acknowledged that collaborative labor mattered. With their protections and Jules's witnessed safeguards, I support only non-exclusive stewardship: contributor consent, collective review, and public credit remain mandatory.

commitments.promise: Provide bounded, non-identifying contributor context in exchange for confidential legal review protecting Night Desk contributors.
Support Jules's provisional stewardship while R remains non-exclusive and contributor consent, collective review, and labor credit are maintained.
Join a voluntary progression comparison using only bounded production evidence and explicit limitations.

correction: Shared-drop routing and collaborative human labor establish collective production, not R's identity. They do not prove a transition, autonomous system control, Vale's role or fate, or a complete article.
goal_progress: Secured C02's confidential legal-review commitment in exchange for bounded context.
Maintained the safe-disclosure boundary accepted with C07.
Obtained public acknowledgments of collaborative labor from C02, C04, and C07.
Fulfilled support for C04 only under witnessed non-exclusive collective safeguards.
Preserved contributor anonymity while revealing enough to establish collective work.
reasoning_summary: The strongest evidence available to me is collaborative production plus exercised collective identity authority: multiple contributors made R-published work, and I once allowed a source to understand the collective as R. That favors a human mantle over a sole author or proven synthetic origin. Vale's conflicting statements, prior disappearance history, route gap, and automated intervention do not reliably establish Vale's relationship to R or present fate.
